task: Evaluate the revised Lalin-native approaches using explicit_programming.md as the governing philosophy. I have read explicit_programming.md fully. The user priority is idiomatic Lalin: the VM design must preserve the dual tree, typed data/control structure, region protocols as documentation, explicit continuations for every meaningful outcome, blocks-as-states, emit/forward composition, explicit persistent state, no hidden C runtime behavior, no stringly exits, no boolean status conventions, no hidden state, no premature functions, and use Lua metaprogramming only for named monomorphic axes of variation. Do not favor porting PUC architecture. Vendored Lua 5.5 is only the semantic/bytecode oracle. Compare Contract-Ladder Region Completion, Explicit Runtime Protocol Spine, and Vertical Semantic Slices on explicit-programming fit, typed data/control clarity, grep-shaped maintainability, compatibility with source+binary Lua 5.5, validator safety, runtime completeness including weak tables/finalizers/userdata __gc/FFI, migration cost, performance risk, and verification. Recommend a path and necessary hybridization, but do not implement.
